title: Carex dianae
taxonomy: Carex dianae is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Central America. It is a part of the genus Carex, which comprises over 2,000 species of sedges. As a member of the Cyperaceae family, it shares characteristics with other sedge species. The specific classification of Carex dianae is further detailed in botanical classifications, typically listed as Carex dianae Kunth. Its classification helps in understanding its growth habits and habitat requirements.

intro: Carex dianae, also known as Diane's sedge or Carex dianae, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Carex dianae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Carex dianae is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, with the most common preparations being decoctions and infusions that can be used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
